title: feat(enter): isolate staging worker to its own tinybird workspace
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Staging worker now writes events to a new dedicated `pollinations_enter_staging` workspace instead of co-mingling with prod (`TINYBIRD_INGEST_TOKEN` and `TINYBIRD_READ_TOKEN` swapped in `secrets/staging.vars.json`)
- Drops the now-redundant `WHERE environment = 'production'` filters from 8 endpoint pipes — the prod workspace no longer receives non-prod traffic, so the filter became a no-op
- Purged ~12.5M historical non-production rows from the prod workspace (one-off cleanup, done out-of-band)
- New staging workspace was created and deployed with the identical schema; both workspaces are in sync as of merge
- Deferred follow-ups (CI checks, auto-deploy, drift check, sync token, 3-tier dev workflow) tracked in #11127

gist.headline: Separating the Staging Swarm
gist.blurb: We've given our staging environment its own dedicated Tinybird workspace. Production analytics are now cleaner, faster, and free of test data.
gist.summary: Isolated the staging worker to a dedicated Tinybird workspace (pollinations_enter_staging) and removed redundant environment filters from 8 production endpoint pipes.
gist.impact: Production analytics queries are cleaner and more efficient now that staging data is routed to its own isolated workspace.
gist.visual_concept: The bee organizing data or separating things into two distinct boxes/workspaces. One box labeled 'PROD', one labeled 'STAGE'. The bee is sorting glowing data crystals.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, sorting glowing data crystals into two separate wooden crates labeled 'PROD' and 'STAGE'. A chunky retro monitor in the background displays clean bar charts. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
